tags.qa_note: Depth corrected to 35m (Aqualonde confirms 35m max; previous value 34m was rounding). Monkfish at eastern face and giant doris confirmed by miopalmoplongee.com.

Casualty count conflict flagged: Wikipedia says 5 deaths; bormesplongee.fr says 2 missing/27 survivors; ulysseplongee.fr says 3 victims. Wikipedia and tribloo dimension data consistent: 78.28m x 11.94m, 1698 GRT, Bergen Mekaniske Verksted confirmed by Wikipedia.

tags.description: Classic Mediterranean wall dive with vertical drop, 10-35m. Posidonia seagrass meadows on shallow plateau. Groupers, morays, and amberjacks in wall crevices. Excellent macro photography and light penetration. 15m+ visibility typical.

tags.qa_note: Sagona dimensions confirmed: 54m long, 8.5m wide, 808 tons, built 1912 by SB Dundee Ltd. Sinking date 3 December 1945 confirmed by 3 independent sources. Casualties 2 dead + 1 missing confirmed by bormesplongee.fr.
White gorgonians (Eunicella singularis) confirmed as defining feature by multiple sources. Depth 5-30m confirmed.

tags.description: Shore-accessible reef in protected marine reserve with snorkeling trail (6 buoys), 1-12m. Beginner-friendly posidonia seagrass exploration. Groupers, bream, and nudibranchs common. Excellent for learning Mediterranean reef ecology.

tags.qa_note: No site-specific sources found in QA passes 2026-05-25 and 2026-06-26. There is a confirmed 'Les Deux Frères' formation near La Seyne-sur-Mer (different location). No Port-Cros operator lists this site by name. Regional characteristics only. Do not mark validated until confirmed by operator-specific source.

The confirmed site near Bagaud island is 'Les Dalles de Bagaud' / 'Dalles de Bagaud' (6-15m, beginner). No source found for a site named 'Pierre de Bagaud' at 25m. These are likely distinct features. Do not mark validated until operator-specific source confirms this exact name and depth.
tags.description: Bagaud island reef (pierre = rock) with rocky formations and posidonia, 5-25m. Intermediate exploration of marine reserve with groupers, octopus, and sea fans. Good visibility (20m+) and moderate currents.
Beach dive site (plage = beach) with easy shore access, depths 2-8m. Beginner-friendly Mediterranean reef with posidonia seagrass beds. Less crowded than La Palud with comparable marine richness.

tags.qa_note: Site exists as a geographic point on Port-Cros (referenced in sea-seek.com and as an alternative dive zone in restricted-area notices), but no dive operator has published specific depth, type, or marine life details. Do not mark validated until operator-specific source confirms.
tags.description: Point (pointe) reef with deeper exploration, 8-30m. Intermediate Mediterranean dive with rocky formations, groupers, and amberjacks. Posidonia and macroalgae. Visibility typically 15-25m with light currents.

tags.name: Wrack 'Sagona' (Le Grec)
tags.qa_note: Alternate OSM node for the Sagona/Le Grec wreck. Same vessel as 'Le Grec Wreck' curated entry. Dimensions confirmed: 54m x 8.5m, 808 tons, built 1912.

tags.name: Wrack 'Prosper Schiaffino' (Alias Donator)
tags.qa_note: Alternate OSM node for the Donator/Prosper Schiaffino. Dimensions confirmed by Wikipedia: 78.28m x 11.94m, 1698 GRT, Bergen Mekaniske Verksted 1931. Casualty count conflict: Wikipedia 5 deaths; bormesplongee.fr 2 missing; ulysseplongee.fr 3 victims. Markdown flags this conflict.

tags.name: La Tantine
tags.qa_note: Vessel type corrected from 'tugboat' to 'crane barge / ponton grue' — it had no motor or propeller. Was towed during construction materials delivery to Port-Cros Manoir hotel (~1960). Depth varies by source: 48m (provencemed, destination.portcros-parcnational.fr), 49m (hyeres-plongee.com), 41m (sun-plongee.com). Using 48m as primary per most sources.

tags.name: Wildcat
tags.qa_note: Origin disputed: bormesplongee.fr and miopalmoplongee.com both cite the 1950s dump-from-carrier theory as more likely. The '15 August 1944 / Operation Dragoon crash' claim in previous markdown was incorrectly attributed to bormesplongee.fr — that source actually favours the 1950s discard theory. Marine life corrected: previous markdown said 'two machine guns in each wing' but all sources cite 6 total machine guns. Engine is missing per miopalmoplongee.com (was omitted from previous description).

tags.name: Gabiniėre Est
tags.qa_note: Difficulty kept as Intermediate per OSM tag; depth range 10-40m confirmed. The 'anglerfish at 28m' claim was removed from markdown — that specific sighting is documented for La Pointe de la Galère (confirmed by miopalmoplongee.com), not for Gabinière Est specifically.

tags.name: l'Esquadillon
tags.qa_note: OSM-tagged as natural reef/islet. No dive-operator specific sources found during QA passes 2026-05-25 and 2026-06-26 confirming depth, site type, or marine life. Description is regional generic.
